Document:
# Status Effects
Status effects are temporary conditions applied to characters through spells or other abilities. They modify behavior, stats, or provide special effects.
## How Status Effects Work
Status effects are applied by certain spells and last for a specific duration. When the duration expires, the effect is removed automatically.
## Applying Status Effects
Most status effects are applied through [bold]spells[/bold]. When you cast a spell that applies a status effect:
1. The spell hits the target 2. Status effect is added with a duration 3. Effect modifies the target's abilities 4. When duration expires, normal abilities return
## Available Status Effects
### Magic Vision - [bold]Description:[/bold] Allows the caster to see magical auras and entities - [bold]Applied by:[/bold] Mana Vision spell - [bold]Duration:[/bold] While active (toggle spell)
### Mana Armor - [bold]Description:[/bold] Consumes mana to reduce incoming damage - [bold]Applied by:[/bold] Mana Armor spell - [bold]Effect:[/bold] -5 mana per hit taken
## Base Game Status Effects
OpenEdge also inherits standard SS14 status effects:
- [bold]Stunned:[/bold] Cannot move or act - [bold]Knockdown:[/bold] Knocked to the ground - [bold]Slowed:[/bold] Reduced movement speed - [bold]Forced Sleeping:[/bold] Unconscious (magical sleep)
## Tips
- [bold]Watch your status icons[/bold] — they show active effects - [bold]Some effects can be cured[/bold] by waiting or using items - [bold]Enemy status effects[/bold] can be exploited — stun them before attacking!
## Related Systems
- [textlink="Magic" link="OE14Magic"] — spells that apply status effects - [textlink="Combat" link="OE14Combat"] — status effects in combat
